business.headline: TCS filing made easier: Income Tax Dept brings in new Form 143, replacing 27EQThe move is part of a wider effort to simplify tax processes under the updated income-tax framework and reduce errors in filing.

Indian delegation to visit US from April 20 as trade talks resumeAn Indian delegation led by the chief negotiator will visit the US from April 20. This will be the first in-person meeting between the two sides in about three to four months.
Sensex ends 1,264 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; IT stocks jumpThe S&P BSE Sensex jumped 1,263.67 points to close at 78,111.24, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 38.65 points to end at 24,231.30.

Is India's gold loan boom quietly building a default risk?Gold loans have grown nearly 3.8 times since March 2022. Their share in the retail credit market has also increased sharply, rising from 5.9% to 11.1% by December 2025. This makes gold loans the second-largest retail loan category after home loans.

DA hike update: Central government staff to protest on April 16Weeks after it was expected, the DA hike for January 2026 is still pending. With no update from the government, central government employee body is preparing to protest on April 16.

Zero tax to pay? 3 big reasons why you should still file your ITRIf your income is low and your taxes come to zero, you might think filing an ITR is not needed. But skipping it could mean missing out on some useful benefits that go beyond just paying tax.

8th Pay Commission: Unions push for Rs 69,000 minimum pay and 6% annual hikeA significant rise in minimum basic pay to Rs 69,000 is at the heart of the demands. The committee has suggested a fitment factor of 3.83, which would substantially raise existing salaries and pensions.

Don't let these 5 mistakes weaken your term insurance coverA term insurance plan can be your family's strongest safety net, but only if it's chosen wisely. Knowing the common mistakes beforehand can help you choose a policy that truly protects your loved ones.

I lost Rs 10,000 in a week as war hit stock markets. Why I didn't stop my SIPsAs the West Asia war sent markets into free fall, I lost Rs 10,000 in a week but didn't stop my SIPs, even when everything on screen told me to. Here's what happened next.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points: 3 reasons why stock market is rising todayAt around 9:48 am, the BSE Sensex was up 1,217.31 points, or 1.58%, at 78,064.88. It opened at 77,981.10 and touched a high of 78,270.42, while the low stood at 77,849.52.
Sensex, Nifty open higher on hopes of renewed US-Iran talksThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 1,189.51 points to 78,037.08, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 366.40 points to 24,209.05 as of 9:26 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market open higher or lower today?Early signals suggest a positive opening. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,228 at 7:34 am. This points to the Nifty 50 opening around 1.6% higher than its last close of 23,842.65 on Monday. Domestic markets were shut on Tuesday due to a public holiday.

Supreme Court closes Sandesara fraud case after Rs 5,000 crore settlementDuring the proceedings, the Sandesara Group paid Rs 3,507 crore directly to the lending consortium, while an additional Rs 1,192 crore was recovered through liquidation. In its compliance order dated December 17, 2025, the Supreme Court recorded a final settlement deposit of Rs 5,111.43 crore.

War-led energy crisis could drag global growth to 2%: IMFAs conflict in West Asia deepens, the IMF warns energy shocks could push global growth toward near-recession levels, exposing fragile economies while inflation remains stubbornly elevated across markets.

IMF raises India FY27 GDP to 6.5% despite global conflict headwindsOne of the key reasons behind the improved outlook is a reduction in additional US tariffs on Indian goods. These tariffs have come down from 50% to 10%, easing pressure on exports.
EPFO new rule: Members can delink wrong Member IDs from UANThe updated system is expected to reduce confusion caused by duplicate or incorrect provident fund accounts.

IEA slashes oil demand and supply view as Iran war rattles markets: ReportThe ongoing conflict in Iran has forced the IEA to revise global oil market predictions, signalling reduced supply and demand growth amid geopolitical tensions.

SIPs are rising, but is retail conviction in equities slowing? Here's the answerRecord SIP numbers suggest confidence, yet other signals point to caution. Retail investors are not exiting equities — they are simply investing with greater care and clarity.

New TRACES portal launched: How will it change your TDS filing experience?The Income Tax Department has rolled out a new TRACES portal aimed at improving usability and speeding up processes. But how much difference will it actually make to your TDS filing experience?

Earn Rs 12 lakh or Rs 30 lakh? See how your gratuity changes with 50% basic payIn Part 1, we explained how take-home may fall despite the same CTC. Here, we look at how the 50% rule impacts long-term benefits like gratuity.

entertainment.headline: Anurag Kashyap opens up on fame, films and surviving the industry Anurag Kashyap discussed the challenges of stardom and his unexpected journey into cinema. He reflected on how passion and adaptability have sustained his long career in the evolving film industry.

Ahaan Panday, Aneet Padda reunite with Saiyaara maker Mohit Suri in musical romance The team behind Saiyaara is reuniting for a new romantic drama, slated for a global release in 2027. Director Mohit Suri and Yash Raj Films team up again with stars Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda.

Amid Jana Nayagan controversy, understanding what leaks do to a film's business The leak of Jana Nayagan has once again highlighted the deep impact of piracy on the film industry, causing both financial loss and emotional strain. Despite action and arrests, the issue persists, pointing to the need for stronger safeguards and greater awareness.

Kartik Aaryan's Naagzilla pushed to 2027? Here's what we know Reports are doing round that the release of Kartik Aaryan's Naagzilla has been postponed. Backed by Dharma Productions and Mahaveer Jain Films, the film was scheduled to hit theatres on Independence Day 2026.

Gulshan Grover: Shatrughan Sinha felt I should've been Rehman Dakait in Dhurandhar Gulshan Grover, in an interview with India Today, reflected on the changes in portraying crime in films and the evolution of villains in Hindi cinema.

Ranbir Kapoor among TIME's 100 most influential people of 2026 Ranbir Kapoor is the only Indian actor to feature on TIME's 100 most influential people list of 2026. His inclusion, along with names like Sundar Pichai and Vikas Khanna, highlights India's growing presence across global culture.

The Hunger Games: Sunrise on the Reaping trailer teases origin story of the saga The first trailer for The Hunger Games: Sunrise on the Reaping is out. Based on Suzanne Collins' record-breaking 2025 novel, the latest instalment serves as a prequel to the original trilogy.

Sidharth Malhotra shares 'girl dad' post wearing a bow, internet calls him cute Sidharth Malhotra gave a sweet peek into fatherhood with a playful post, fully embracing his "girl dad" era. His moments with daughter Saraayah feel warm and real, capturing the quiet, everyday joy of being a parent.

The Mummy: Director Lee Cronin aims to deliver a slow-burn horror thriller Director Lee Cronin promises to present a slow-burn psychological thriller with his new film, The Mummy, focusing on atmosphere and emotional dread.

Varun Tej undergoes knee surgery amid Bhari prep, Niharika shares health update Actor Varun Tej underwent surgery after suffering a knee injury while preparing for his upcoming film, Bhari. His sister, Niharika Konidela, confirmed he was recovering and thanked fans for their support.

Peddi update: New release window of Ram Charan film revealed. Here's what we know The new release window of Ram Charan's upcoming film, Peddi has been announced. The decision has been made to allow more time for technical and post-production improvements.

Akshay Kumar remembers Asrani ahead of Bhooth Bangla release: Master of comedy Akshay Kumar wrote an emotional note remembering Asrani ahead of Bhooth Bangla release, calling it a tribute to the late legend.

Pallichattambi review: Tovino Thomas fights hard, but the script doesn'tRelease Date:Wed Apr 15, 2026 Pallichattambi movie review: Director Dijo Jose Antony's Pallichattambi, starring Tovino Thomas, Kayadu Lohar and Vijayaraghavan, is a routine drama on feudalism and approach. While the film concentrates on scale, it struggles in terms of treatment.

Interview: K-pop group AMPERS&ONE Open Up on Music, Brotherhood, India & What's Next K-pop group AMPERS&ONE are stepping into their 'Definition' era with clarity and ambition. In an exclusive chat with Bhavna Agarwal for India Today, the group opens up about shaping their fourth mini-album, from lyricism to concept building, and the striking choreography behind 'God'.
Prime Video, ZEE5 this week: Matka King, Assi and more Prime Video and ZEE5 bring a varied lineup this week with titles like Matka King, Assi and American Gladiators.

Vijay Varma reveals why he rejected multiple serial killer roles post-Dahaad After his acclaimed turn in Dahaad, Vijay Varma chose to avoid serial killer roles to prevent repetition and explore diverse characters. Speaking to us, he shared he wanted to break free from dark characters.

You need a new prescription: Meryl Streep's witty remark to KJo steals the show Filmmaker Karan Johar recently sat down with Hollywood icons Meryl Streep and Anne Hathaway to discuss their upcoming film, The Devil Wears Prada 2. During the conversation Streep's witty remark stole the spotlight.
Netflix this week: Do Deewane Seher Mein, Ustaad Bhagat Singh and more Netflix brings a diverse lineup this week with titles like BEEF Season 2, Stranger Things spin-off, and regional films.

Game of Thrones: Aegon's Conquest film to be written by Andor fame Beau Willimon The new film from George RR Martin's Game of Thrones universe has been officially titled Aegon's Conquest. It is written by Andor fame Beau Willimon.

general news.headline: Iran, resistance stand as one in war and truce, Tehran warns US of Israel firstIran said a comprehensive Lebanon ceasefire would stem from Hezbollah's resilience and Resistance unity, insisting Washington must uphold the agreement. Tehran warned the US to abandon its "Israel first" approach, saying Iran and the Resistance remain inseparable, including in ceasefire.

US confirms costliest air loss in Iran war: A drone twice the price of F-35 jetA $240-million US surveillance drone, worth more than two F-35 fighter jets, vanished over the Persian Gulf. The US Navy confirmed it crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, fuelling speculation that Iran might have taken down yet another American aerial asset.

Iran signals Hormuz shift: Free passage via Oman on table in US talksThe plan signals a softer tone from Tehran after weeks of hardline rhetoric, though uncertainty over mines, access rules and US response keeps the situation fragile.

White House says second round of US-Iran talks likely to be held in PakistanIslamabad has turned into the front line of diplomacy, with Washington looking to return there for fresh negotiations. Karoline Leavitt described Pakistan as the "only mediator" in the current negotiations, praising its role in facilitating dialogue between the two sides.
The delimitation outrage explainedWhy should your vote count the same as someone who didn't bother to stop at two children?

Live: Airbus Space likely shared imagery of US military assets with China before Operation Epic FuryUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Tensions in the Middle East remain on edge as diplomacy and military pressure move in parallel, with Pakistan once again stepping in as a mediator. Pakistan's Army Chief Asim Munir has arrived in Tehran carrying messages between Washington and Tehran after talks in Islamabad failed to yield a breakthrough, even as Iranian officials confirmed that "several messages have been exchanged through Pakistan" since the last round. The United States, meanwhile, has tightened its naval blockade on Iranian ports, claiming it has "completely halted economic trade going into and out of Iran by sea", prompting a sharp warning from Tehran that it could block shipping routes across the Red Sea, the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman if pressure continues. At the same time, US President Donald Trump has struck an optimistic tone, saying the war is "close to over" and hinting that fresh talks could take place in Pakistan in the coming days, though no timeline has been confirmed. The fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, set to expire soon, adds urgency to the situation, even as parallel tensions persist elsewhere in the region, including ongoing exchanges between Israel and Hezbollah and discussions around a possible Lebanon truce. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

Rain coming: Multiple western disturbances to hit India on this dateThe weather systems follow many western disturbances that hit India recently, causing rain and hailstorms. Some states have received much-needed relief in the form of cooler temperatures at a time when the summer heat is almost here.

Fake it to stay: Pakistani, Bangladeshi migrants coached to pose as gay for UK asylumThe BBC investigation finds migrants allegedly coached to fake asylum claims in the UK, with advisers charging thousands, raising concerns over systemic abuse and prompting calls for a government crackdown.

When a Maharaja carried 8,000 litres of Ganga water to LondonHow the faith of an Indian Maharaja travelled across seas, allowing him to carry Gangajal all the way to London, remains one of the more telling stories of faith and orthodoxism in early 20th-century India. The world's largest silver jars now stand preserved in the City Palace of Jaipur, drawing the attention of almost every visitor who walks past them.

8th Pay Commission: Unions push for Rs 69,000 minimum pay and 6% annual hikeA significant rise in minimum basic pay to Rs 69,000 is at the heart of the demands. The committee has suggested a fitment factor of 3.83, which would substantially raise existing salaries and pensions.

Deal or delay? US, Iran weigh two-week ceasefire extension before deadlineProgress in US–Iran talks raises hopes of a deal, as officials narrow differences ahead of a ceasefire deadline, though key issues remain unresolved and a final agreement is not guaranteed.

About to eliminate: Netanyahu says Israel closing in on Hezbollah strongholdNetanyahu says Israeli forces are closing in on Hezbollah's stronghold in Bint Jbeil, while continuing strikes and negotiations with Lebanon, as ceasefire pressure grows and regional tensions remain high.

Despite action and arrests, the issue persists, pointing to the need for stronger safeguards and greater awareness.

R Vaishali becomes first Indian woman to win Candidates Chess, to fight for world titleR Vaishali clinched the 2026 FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament on Wednesday, earning the right to challenge China's reigning champion Ju Wenjun for the Women's World Chess Championship crown later this year.

African Catholics recoil as Trump's Jesus post and spat with Pope deepen tensionsTensions have been building since late March, when Pope Leo — the first US-born leader of the Catholic Church — began speaking out against the Iran conflict following US-Israeli strikes. His stance has put him at odds with Trump, who has responded with increasingly pointed criticism.

Asim Munir in Iran to deliver US's message after Islamabad talks failPakistan's military confirmed that Asim Munir, along with Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi, is part of the delegation now in Iran. The visit is meant to convey messages between the two sides and help set the stage for another round of talks, which could once again be held in Islamabad.

Why Trump cannot restart Iran war easily nowPresident Trump's refusal to extend the Iran ceasefire risks renewal of the conflict. But falling approval, economic strain, and backlash over his feud with Pope Leo XIV are fracturing MAGA support. And despite all his threats, Trump might find it very difficult to strike Iran given the high political stakes amid the midterm elections.

IPL 2026: Ruthless RCB blow away hapless LSG to top the tableRoyal Challengers Bengaluru climbed to the top of the table with a commanding win, handling a tricky home surface with assurance to outplay Lucknow Super Giants. Chasing a modest target, RCB wrapped up a five-wicket victory with 29 balls to spare, capping off a dominant all-round performance.

Did TCS Nashik fail its women employees? Case points to huge POSH gapsTCS Nashik unit's sexual harassment case has triggered scrutiny of workplace grievance mechanisms. The FIRs alleged that repeated complaints went unaddressed and pointed to possible lapses in POSH implementation. Multiple arrests, including a POSH-linked official, have shifted focus to how internal systems function when complaints are raised.

Iran to execute 1st woman anti-regime protester as crackdown on unrest continuesBita Hemmati was tried alongside her husband, Mohammadreza Majid Asl, 34, and two other men, Behrouz and Kourosh Zamaninezhad, all of whom were handed death sentences in the same case.

Omar Abdullah refuses to cut Tricolour ribbon at event, video goes viralThe incident took place at the inaugural ceremony of Know Your Artisan, an initiative by the Jammu and Kashmir government aimed at promoting the region's handicrafts and artistic heritage, under the 'Soulful Kashmir' campaign.

Strait of Hormuz blockade showdown: Here's how US and China's militaries stack upThe United States has intensified its naval blockade in the Gulf of Oman, forcing a sanctioned Chinese tanker to make a U-turn after it attempted to exit the Strait of Hormuz.

Can't back out after divorce settlement: Supreme Court lays down lawThe Supreme Court draws a firm line on mutual divorce settlements, ruling against last-minute withdrawals and flagging misuse of legal remedies, while also quashing a domestic violence case it found to be an afterthought.

Student shoots 9 dead at Turkey school before killing self; 2nd incident in 2 daysA day after 16 people were injured in a mass shooting at a Turkey school, a student opened fire at another high school in the country, killing nine people and injuring 13 others. The attacker later killed himself.

sports.headline: KKR should open with Green: Aaron Finch on all-rounder's struggles in IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Cameron Green has struggled since returning from injury and being used out of position, with poor performances in both batting and bowling adding to KKR's concerns.

Not as versatile as Bumrah, but as effective: Behind Josh Hazlewood's IPL success Royal Challengers Bengaluru fast bowler Josh Hazlewood admitted he does not possess the same range of variations as Jasprit Bumrah, but said his success in the Indian Premier League has come from sticking to accuracy and a simple method.

Virat Kohli embraces modern T20 approach, but keeps focus on smart cricket Virat Kohli said he is constantly looking to raise the bar when it comes to adapting to the modern ways of T20 cricket. However, the RCB star stressed that smart cricket is also needed after the win over LSG.

Bittersweet moment: Watch Pragg celebrate sister Vaishali's historic Candidates win An emotional R Vaishali celebrated with her family members after winning the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament, sealing a historic title and a place in the World Championship. For R Praggnanandhaa, the celebrations carried a bittersweet note after his disappointing campaign in the tournament.

IPL 2026: Ruthless RCB blow away hapless LSG to top the table Royal Challengers Bengaluru climbed to the top of the table with a commanding win, handling a tricky home surface with assurance to outplay Lucknow Super Giants. Chasing a modest target, RCB wrapped up a five-wicket victory with 29 balls to spare, capping off a dominant all-round performance.

Carlos Alcaraz pulls out of Barcelona Open, days after losing No.1 ranking Carlos Alcaraz has withdrawn from the Barcelona Open due to a wrist injury, ending his chances of reclaiming the world No.1 ranking. His exit comes days after losing the top spot to Jannik Sinner, who beat him in the Monte Carlo Masters final.

R Vaishali becomes first Indian woman to win Candidates Chess, to fight for world title R Vaishali clinched the 2026 FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament on Wednesday, earning the right to challenge China's reigning champion Ju Wenjun for the Women's World Chess Championship crown later this year.

No late heroics: Rishabh Pant's brave return from injury ends in disappointment IPL 2026, RCB vs LSG: Rishabh Pant showed grit by returning to bat after an elbow injury but failed to make an impact, managing just one run in the match between Lucknow Super Giants and Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

IPL 2026: CSK face MS Dhoni dilemma as return raises impact sub question MS Dhoni's likely return has presented Chennai Super Kings with a selection dilemma just as the side has begun to build momentum in IPL 2026. Former Australia captain Aaron Finch has suggested that Dhoni could be utilised in a more defined, situation-based role, including as an Impact Player, amid concerns over his recent struggles with batting position and finishing games.

Rohit ruled out? MI to wait until PBKS matchday to take call on availability Mumbai Indians are set to wait until match day to make a call about Rohit Sharma's availability for the clash against Punjab Kings on Thursday, April 16. Rohit suffered the injury during the loss to RCB.

IPL 2026, RCB vs LSG: Kohli overcomes injury scare, but named Impact player Virat Kohli has shaken off an injury concern to be ready to play the game against Lucknow Super Giants at the Chinnaswamy stadium on Wednesday, April 15. However, Kohli has been named as the impact substitute for the game.

Can anyone say no to Virat, Rohit? Munaf backs Gautam Gambhir's hardline styleMunaf Patel said Gautam Gambhir's willingness to say no to icons like Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma has helped maintain order during a tricky transition period, warning that Indian cricket would collapse without his authority.

Ben Stokes lucky to be alive after nasty facial injury: Thankfully, still hereBen Stokes said that he is happy to be alive after suffering a horrific facial injury in February while training with his county side Durham. Stokes was hit on the face by a stray ball and had to undergo surgery to repair the injury.

Hugo Ekitike's World Cup hopes hit after injury in Liverpool lossHugo Ekitike is set for a lengthy spell on the sidelines after suffering a suspected Achilles tendon rupture during Liverpool's defeat to Paris Saint-Germain. The injury comes as a major blow to both Liverpool's season and France's World Cup hopes, especially given his impressive return of 19 goals this campaign.

Josh Inglis for Nicholas Pooran? Irfan Pathan flags key concern for LSGIPL 2026: Irfan Pathan has said Nicholas Pooran may be under pressure at Lucknow Super Giants after a poor IPL run and could be replaced by Josh Inglis, when the Australian joins the camp. Pooran has scored just 41 runs in four innings at a strike rate of 85.41.

Sunil Chhetri's 12th man duty for Mumbai Indians goes viral for his catching skillsIPL 2026: Sunil Chhetri made a special appearance at the Mumbai Indians' training session at Wankhede Stadium, taking part in a catching drill and sharing a light-hearted moment with the squad. He also received a personalised jersey from skipper Hardik Pandya.

RCB vs LSG Highlights: All-round RCB thrash LSG, move to top of the tableRoyal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) vs Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) Highlights, IPL 2026: It's been a clinical performance with the bat and ball as RCB thrash LSG by five wickets to make it to the top of the table. The bowlers set up the win and then Virat Kohli and Rajat Patidar ensured that the team got over the line in the end.

Overcoming Bumrah made other Indian bowlers easy to face: Sahibzada FarhanPakistan batter Sahibzada Farhan opened up on facing Jasprit Bumrah, saying that taking down the Indian pacer gave him the confidence to face the rest of the Indian attack. Farhan has hit Bumrah for three sixes in T20Is.

Hazlewood and Duffy together? How Impact Subs can solve RCB selection puzzleHanuma Vihari explains how RCB can use the Impact Player rule to field both Josh Hazlewood and Jacob Duffy, while managing overseas balance in IPL 2026.

Only retired IPL stars come to PSL: Ahmed Shahzad gives Pakistan reality-checkAhmed Shehzad criticised PSL's approach to banning players joining IPL, urging focus on growth as PCB penalised Muzarabani and others for withdrawing from PSL contracts.

IPL 2026: Dewald Brevis backs 'special' Ayush Mhatre, feels spark in CSK campDewald Brevis praised Ayush Mhatre as "special" for his fearless batting. Brevis also expressed confidence after returning from injury, adding depth to the side following his match-winning contribution against Kolkata Knight Riders.

Big blow for KKR? On-air blunder after Gaikwad's dismissal leaves fans in spiltsMichael Clarke's "big blow for KKR" remark after Ruturaj Gaikwad's dismissal sparked reactions, with fans highlighting the irony during CSK's win over KKR in IPL 2026.

Should CSK be worried about Ruturaj Gaikwad's poor start?IPL 2026: Ruturaj Gaikwad, who has been one of the most consistent performers in the Indian Premier League, has had a rough start to the new season. The Chennai Super Kings captain has scored only 63 runs in five matches.

Viktor Axelsen retires at 32 in shock call, explains decision in emotional Insta postTwo-time Olympic champion Viktor Axelsen announced his retirement from professional badminton on Tuesday, April 15. The 32-year-old from Denmark said he was not able to carry on due to recurring back injury issues.

Rinku Singh overhyped? Ex-captain says KKR star not good enough for No. 5 roleIPL 2026: Former India captain Kris Srikkanth said Rinku Singh is not suited for the No. 5 role, criticising Kolkata Knight Riders' decision-making after their 32-run loss to Chennai Super Kings. KKR remain the only winless side this season, with four defeats and just one point from a rain-affected game.

IPL 2026: RCB sweat on Virat Kohli's fitness, seek table-topping win vs LSGIPL 2026, RCB vs LSG: Virat Kohli will be in focus after his injury as Royal Challengers Bengaluru get set to lock horns with the Lucknow Super Giants on Wednesday at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru.

KKR power coach Andre Russell could've done more than Rs 25 crore Cameron Green: KaifMohammad Kaif slammed KKR's decision to sideline Andre Russell and back Rs 25 crore Cameron Green after another loss, as the team remains winless in IPL 2026.

CSK vs KKR: How MS Dhoni's words sparked Noor Ahmad's turnaround at ChepaukCSK vs KKR: Noor Ahmad starred with 3/21 as CSK beat KKR, with bowling coach Sriram revealing how MS Dhoni's advice and Akeal Hosein's inputs helped the spinner find his rhythm at Chepauk.

Barcelona come undone as Atletico Madrid return to Champions League semifinalsFC Barcelona's early dominance wasn't enough as Atletico Madrid advanced to the UEFA Champions League semifinals, continuing Barca's struggles in Europe after another knockout exit.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's code cracked by an IPL rookie. Will others follow?IPL 2026: SRH debutant Praful Hinge removed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi with a perfectly executed hard-length delivery, aimed squarely at a well-known vulnerability. The plan was no secret, but it was Hinge's precision and discipline that set him apart from others who had tried and failed.

Play of the Day: Ruturaj Gaikwad's Noor ploy scripts historic low for KKRKolkata Knight Riders lost their fourth game of the IPL 2026 season after falling to Chennai Super Kings in Chepauk. While Noor won the Player of the Match award for his sensational spell, Ruturaj Gaikwad's captaincy perhaps did not get the accolades it deserved.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
